calendar.matters: Notice Of Motion To Be Relieved As Counsel

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Final Distribution
Off Calendar Feb-25-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Ryan Finn, via ZOOM, for Patricia Amaro, Personal Representative; Mac Cabal, via CourtCall, for American Contractors Indemnity.

The court begins by asking counsel if notice has been provided to Michael Grillo. Counsel reports that he has made several attempts to contact Mr. Grillo to no avail. The court then asks if Mr. Grillo's distribution will go to the treasurer is he cannot be located. Counsel replies that he is unsure but was hoping to receive guidance from the court regarding the preceding. The court and counsel discuss the several issues regarding Ms. Amaro making payments and settling claims without court approval. Counsel acknowledges that Ms. Amaro made those mistakes but reports that she made them all in good faith. The court and counsel discuss the difference between the Inventory and Appraisal showing more than $70,000 in assets and the bank statement showing more than $103,000. Counsel reports that the was an IRA account of about $30,000 which listed Ms. Amaro as the sole beneficiary, which was liquidated. The Court noted that it was told that Ms. Amaro made two distributions without Court approval and that there is $76,000.00 in a bank account. The Court also stated that it wants to know whether or not that money sat in the bank account throughout the estate process, whether Ms. Amaro took it out and then put it back in, and that it wants to see whether she can continue to carry out her Fiduciary obligations. Mr. Nelson stated that he does not think that anything they said on Ms. Amaro's behalf was intentional or misleading, that she doesn't believe a Court order was needed when doing that distribution when she had independent authority, that he would not have told her to do that, but another Attorney did, and that Ms. Amaro had to make those distributions in order to deal with the law suits in New York. The Court raised the issue of the inventory and appraisal not being done appropriately, the securities needing to be appraised by a Probate Referee and notice not going out to the Decedent's Mother's Family. Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside And Vacate Order Surcharging And Removing Administrator
Off Calendar Jul-17-2019 Continued To Sep-11-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Evan C. Nelson, Esq. for Patricia Nelson, Petitioner.
Shashauna M. Szczechowicz, Esq. appeared by CourtCall for American Contractors Indemnity Co., Interested Party.

The Court stated that the parties are here on Ms. Amaro's motion to vacate the "unvacated balance" of the Court's order removing her as the administrator. The Court addressed the extensive efforts that it made in the orders to appear that were an attempt to try to get Ms. Amaro to comply. The Court also addressed the merits of Code of Civil Procedure 473 which rely on the motion being accompanied by a pleading which Ms. Amaro did not include. The Court presented a brief timeline and noted that Ms. Amaro did nothing between 2008 and 2019 noting that this was an eleven year period of time where nothing at all was done. The Court stated that it has concerns that Ms. Amaro is the appropriate person that should be appointed. The Court stated that Ms. Amaro filed nothing more than an Inventory and Appraisal which indicated that there was $105,000.00 in cash. The Court further addressed the possibility of there being other beneficiaries and Ms. Amaro's petition that states that she is the only beneficiary entitled to 100%. Argument by Mr. Nelson is held. Mr. Nelson stated that he didn't have the orders to appear, that there was a serious breakdown in communication, that Ms. Amaro had two attorneys: one here and one in New York, that his office is now involved to direct/assist Ms. Amaro, that Ms. Amaro does have a brother and a sister, that her attorney told her to make distributions to them, that there's paperwork to support the distributions. and that there isn't anyone remaining that hasn't had a rightful distribution. Ms. Szczechowicz argued that the surety filed their own motion to set aside the surcharge, that the surety is hampered & needs an accounting, and that they support Mr. Nelson's request to get the documents & close this estate.

Argument concluded. The Court stated that it was continuing the hearing to provide an opportunity by Ms. Amaro to file a declaration with the attached pleading that should request that the Court approve the status of administration & allow continued administration. Ms. Amaro's declaration should address the $105,000.00, that she can address the $105,000.00 without bank records, and that the declaration should also set forth what Ms. Amaro has done. The Court noted that it was trying to get Ms. Amaro's attention and that apparently it now has her attention.

1 - Patricia Amaro's Notice of Motion And Motion To Set Aside And Vacate Order Surcharging And Removing Administrator on calendar today is continued to September 11, 2019 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204. Ms. Amaro's declaration must be on file by August 28, 2019.
2 - The Hearing On The Following Issues: (A) Whether To Impose A Surcharge And (B) The Amount Of Any Such Surcharge currently on calendar for July 30, 2019 is continued to September 11, 2019 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204.
3 - Patricia Amaro's Verified Petition Seeking Order Determining Patricia Hand Amaro As Rightful Heir Of Decedents Estate Pursuant To Probate Code 11700 on calendar for September 4, 2019 is continued to September 11, 2019 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204.
4 - At the request of Ms. Szczechowicz, the American Contractors Indemnity Co.'s July briefing deadline is continued to August 28, 2019.

The Court stated that it would see where we are at that point. The Court noted that if Ms. Amaro is not claiming 100% then an amendment may need to be filed, that there should be timely statutory notice, and if that is the case that notice should be given as broadly as possible. Mr. Nelson stated that there really isn't anyone else out there and that they would chart the genealogy. The Court reiterated that if there needs to be an amendment that it needs to be fully noticed.
